reviews.author: Fred Jaeger
reviews.text: There are two entrances. The west gate was closed when we arrived, but they still tried to charge a parking fee. Go to the east entrance instead. Car parking was 20k VND, entry ticket 50k and motorbike 50k. The lake is the highlight and absolutely worth visiting, especially if you have a bike. Riding around it feels peaceful and scenic. In the north part of the lake there is a stone wall/path you can walk on which makes for great photos. We did two full rounds because it was so relaxing. More local people visiting than foreigners. You see far more Vietnamese visitors than foreigners, many dressed up for photos, which actually adds to the atmosphere rather than making it crowded. Definitely a must-visit in Ninh Binh.

reviews.author: John Winter
reviews.text: Just 8km from Ninh Binh town, I took a quick Grab ride out here and it was absolutely worth it. The place is breathtakingly beautiful—surrounded by lush limestone cliffs and tranquil water, it feels like a hidden paradise. The entrance tunnel is vibrant and colorful, setting the tone for what’s inside. There’s a peaceful path that loops around the lake, ideal for a scenic walk or cycling (you can rent single or tandem bikes onsite). One of the highlights was discovering the small cave temple near the shops—definitely take a few moments to explore inside, it’s quiet and atmospheric. The whole area is extremely photogenic, with plenty of Instagram-worthy spots along the lake and in front of the temple. Overall, a perfect blend of natural beauty and cultural charm. Highly recommend if you’re looking for a peaceful escape from town.

reviews.author: Guillaume
reviews.text: Nice place for a walk. I didn't want to climb the 174 steps of the stairs leasing to the cave, but walking around the lake was nice. The decor with high rocks surrounding the lake is majestuous. You can only enter walking or with bicycle, it's a very good idea to forbid motorbike and electric bus. There are some place to sit and drink smoothies or est snack. It is quite an enjoyable place if the weather is good.

reviews.author: Jesse Bozinis
reviews.text: Beautiful spot but it should not cost 50k to visit Classic tourist trap vibes , pay to park your bike then pay again to rent a bike for a little 2km walk around the lake Also the online pictures are deceptive as the back door of the main pagoda are now permanently closed The bike ticket collector also tried to hold our bikes hostage unless we could provide a rental ticket to LEAVE! despite the bikes having clear branding of the homestay they were from. So keep your parking ticket. Great location . Poor handling of the experience

reviews.author: Vu Phong
reviews.text: An honest review from a Vietnamese A 15-20 mins walk around the lake, the following point of interests can be discovered during the stroll: the pagoda, the citadel's stone door *which is closed indefinitely, not what you would expect from the photos, the cave & the exculpate well inside, fyi the name comes from a local legend, you can ask your tour guy for more detail. A 200 climbs is required to reach the top, bewildering the visitors with a spectacular overall view of the place. However, the experience didn't justify the tickets price, which increased to 50k *compared to 20k before. You can also rent a bicycle here, but aren't allowed to use your own bike, which is a classic tourist trap. Just avoid hassling with the locals if you really wanted to visit the location.

reviews.author: T DN
reviews.text: So beautiful landscapes! 50k/ pax for ticket! Enjoy the sugar can juice so good (20k)

reviews.author: tuan nguyen
reviews.text: When visiting here, you should open wiki to read this amazing and real historical story of the Queen Duong Van Nga, the King Dinh Tien Hoang, the King Le Dai Hanh and the Princess Le Thi Phat Ngan. Why they built and used this place during many periods. The true history will make you feel magnificent and breathless.

reviews.author: Rahul Kotabage
reviews.text: It’s a small place and pushed up more as something for tourists… Views are beautiful though from the top… getting there is another challenge though with some 200 steps going up… view is spectacular… You can take bike to ride in and they are pretty good bikes compared to lots of places… Though the price is bit too high for what’s on offer

reviews.author: Nguyen Duc Tien
reviews.text: a small cave, till have pure water is dropping from the top. The story goes that King Dinh Tien Hoang fed tigers and kept prisoners here to punish them.

reviews.author: Glenn Bancroft
reviews.text: Beautiful spot. You go through a short tunnel and there is an amazing lake with a path around it. Hiring a bike will make it easy as it is all flat. The steps to the cave are steep and a little rough if you don't have good legs but not far

reviews.author: Donghwan Lim
reviews.text: The scenery is nice and walking is nice.

reviews.author: Mohona Paul
reviews.text: Beautiful place. Totally worth it!

reviews.author: Jelly Pie
reviews.text: For shooting only, beautiful place.

reviews.author: Bartosz Sokołowski
reviews.text: You have to leave your bike outside to rent another bike inside. That is of course if you manage to communicate with the lady selling tickets.

reviews.author: Misc SaSi
reviews.text: This is a place to take Instagram photos, nothing more. If that's what you want, then come here (it's 50k dong). If you want something more authentic and natural, then skip this.

reviews.author: Sun B.
reviews.text: A good place to get lost in Ninh Binh. Peaceful and the view is awesome

reviews.author: Amotz Harari
reviews.text: A sweet little atraction for a 10-15 minute quiet strole arround the mou tain enclised lake, see the Vietnam king's little cliff pagoda and the now bhdist cave with a sacred clearwater pool where he used to throw in offenders to feed them to a mithical dragon, Giai, after whom the cave is named.

reviews.author: Robby Fiveboy
reviews.text: Absolutely like visiting paradise. End of story.

reviews.author: MARTIN
reviews.text: Awesome nice lake Picturesque scenery Very unfriendly ticket lady Entry fee: 100 000 VND Bike parking: 5 000 VND Cave: Not allowed going with your own bike inside! (Stupid) - but it is anyway better walking inside than biking

reviews.author: Ondine C
reviews.text: Le site est très beau quand il y a peu de monde. C’est très paisible. Vous pouvez louer un vélo ou un tandem mais ce n’est pas obligatoire. Vous pourrez faire le tour du site à pied en prenant un peu plus le temps. Le point de vue de la Pagode est joli.

reviews.author: Marcel T.
reviews.text: Unbedingt besuchen- Ein Ort der Stille und inneren Einkehr Dong Am Tieng ist wahrlich ein verstecktes Juwel und eine Oase der Ruhe. Abseits des Trubels und eingebettet in üppiges Grün strahlt dieser Ort eine tiefe spirituelle Kraft und Gelassenheit aus. Bereits beim Ankommen spürt man die besondere Atmosphäre – still, friedlich und nahezu zeitlos. Die Architektur ist schlicht, aber eindrucksvoll, und fügt sich harmonisch in die Natur ein. Überall begegnet man kleinen Details, die zum Verweilen und Nachdenken einladen. Es ist ein idealer Ort, um zur Ruhe zu kommen, den eigenen Gedanken Raum zu geben oder einfach nur die stille Schönheit der Umgebung zu genießen. Wer inneren Frieden sucht oder einen Moment der Entschleunigung fernab des Alltags, wird Dong Am Tieng als heilsamen Rückzugsort erleben.

reviews.author: Maxim Khrustalev
reviews.text: Наикрасивейший пейзаж озера в ущелье между горами. Берега облагородили. С одной стороны на горе пагода с пещерой отделанной для молитв и подземный озером, с другой стороны башня охранчющая вход в другую долину. Не повезло, что совпал по времени с толпой школьников и известной тик-ток личностью. Все вместе создал гам и поломоло впечатление. Ещё больше поломала жадность персонала. Не дают въехать на своём велосипеде - нужно оплатить парковку и арендовать такой же местный вел. Деньги небольшие, но впечатление портит

reviews.author: 정상호
reviews.text: 닌빈여행중 이 호수가 조용하게 한바퀴 산책했다. 나는 좋았다. 입장료 50000동 다른 사원들 보는거 보다 여기서 더 차분하게 힐링을 했다. 👍 자전거는 티켓룸 옆에 있다

reviews.author: Bơ
reviews.text: Giá vé vào cửa 50k/ người, thuê xe đạp thường 50k/ xe, xe đạp đôi thì 100k. Nên thuê xe đạp để đi quanh hồ. Không có nhiều điểm xem hay chơi trong này đâu, có 1 cái hồ nước to thui. Hôm nào mát mẻ đạp xe chill chill rồi chụp ảnh rất xinh, hôm nào nóng thì ôi thôi lun

reviews.author: Phương Chi Lê
reviews.text: Giá vé vào cửa 50k/ người, thuê xe đạp thường 50k/ xe, xe đạp đôi thì 100k. Nên thuê xe đạp để đi quanh hồ. Không có nhiều điểm xem hay chơi nhưng view chụp từ động Am Tiên cũng khá đẹp

reviews.author: Hào Nguyễn Thế
reviews.text: Leo lên động Âm Tiên nhìn từ trên cao khá đẹp
